title: bromus lanceolatus subsp. macrostachys
intro: Bromus lanceolatus subsp. macrostachys, also known as meadow brome, is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of meadow brome include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Meadow brome can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
